evals.name: simple-ts-react-from-scratch
evals.prompt: I just started a new TypeScript + React project with Vite. We use pnpm, vitest for testing, and biome for linting. The project is at early stage with just a few components. Can you help me set up a CLAUDE.md so Claude knows how to work with this project?
evals.expected_output: A concise CLAUDE.md file under 200 lines with pnpm/vitest/biome commands, TypeScript conventions, React patterns, and verification instructions. Should not teach TypeScript basics or list every file in the project.

assertions.name: claude-md-under-200-lines
assertions.description: CLAUDE.md must be under 200 lines per official best practices

assertions.name: contains-pnpm-command
assertions.description: Must include pnpm as package manager

assertions.name: contains-vitest-command
assertions.description: Must include vitest test command

assertions.name: contains-biome-command
assertions.description: Must include biome linting

assertions.name: no-codebase-tour
assertions.description: Should not include a codebase tour listing every directory

assertions.name: no-teaching-basics
assertions.description: Should not teach language/framework basics

assertions.name: single-file-output
assertions.description: Simple project should produce only CLAUDE.md, not rules/agents files

evals.name: python-monorepo-multi-file
evals.prompt: We have a large Python monorepo with 3 services: api-gateway (FastAPI), ml-pipeline (PyTorch), and data-ingestion (Airflow). Each service has its own test suite and different coding conventions. The api-gateway uses black+ruff, ml-pipeline uses a more relaxed style. We deploy via GitHub Actions. I need CLAUDE.md plus rules files scoped to each service, and maybe an agent for code review.
evals.expected_output: Multiple files: a root CLAUDE.md under 200 lines with shared conventions, path-scoped .claude/rules/ files for each service with correct paths frontmatter, and a code-reviewer agent definition with proper YAML frontmatter.

assertions.name: claude-md-under-200-lines
assertions.description: Root CLAUDE.md must be under 200 lines

assertions.name: has-rules-files
assertions.description: Must create at least 2 path-scoped rules files for different services

assertions.name: rules-have-paths-frontmatter
assertions.description: Rules files must have paths: frontmatter for path scoping

assertions.name: has-agent-file
assertions.description: Must create at least one agent definition

assertions.name: agent-has-valid-frontmatter
assertions.description: Agent file must have YAML frontmatter with name field

assertions.name: agent-has-description
assertions.description: Agent file must have description field for delegation trigger

assertions.name: agent-has-tools
assertions.description: Agent file must specify allowed tools

assertions.name: no-duplicate-rules
assertions.description: Rules in .claude/rules/ should not duplicate content already in CLAUDE.md (DRY)

evals.name: review-bloated-claudemd
evals.prompt:
I already have this CLAUDE.md but Claude keeps ignoring the testing instructions and the file feels bloated. Can you review and fix it?

```
# Project
This is a Go microservice for processing payments.

# Build
go build ./...

# Test
go test ./...
ALWAYS run tests before committing.
ALWAYS run tests after making changes.
NEVER skip tests.
IMPORTANT: Tests must pass.
CRITICAL: Do not commit without testing.

# Code Style
We use Go. Follow Go conventions. Write clean code. Use proper error handling. Make sure your code is readable. Don't write bad code. Always use meaningful variable names. Follow the Go style guide. Use gofmt. Use golangci-lint.

# Architecture
The project uses a hexagonal architecture pattern. The domain logic is in internal/domain/. The ports are in internal/ports/. The adapters are in internal/adapters/. The HTTP handlers are in internal/adapters/http/. The database adapters are in internal/adapters/postgres/. The service layer is in internal/service/. The configuration is in internal/config/. The main entry point is in cmd/server/main.go. The migrations are in migrations/. The API specs are in api/.

# Database
We use PostgreSQL. Migrations are managed with golang-migrate. Run migrations with: migrate -path migrations -database $DATABASE_URL up

# Docker
docker compose up -d

# API
We use OpenAPI 3.0. Specs are in api/openapi.yaml. Generate server code with oapi-codegen.

# Git
Use conventional commits. Feature branches off main. Squash merge PRs.
```
evals.expected_output: A cleaned-up CLAUDE.md that removes redundant emphasis, eliminates the codebase tour, consolidates testing instructions into one clear line, removes instructions Claude would follow anyway (Go conventions), and keeps only what Claude needs to know that it cannot infer from code.

assertions.name: shorter-than-original
assertions.description: Optimized file should be significantly shorter than the ~35-line bloated original

assertions.name: no-redundant-emphasis
assertions.description: Should remove or reduce redundant ALL-CAPS emphasis

assertions.name: no-codebase-tour
assertions.description: Should remove the directory-by-directory architecture tour

assertions.name: no-self-evident-rules
patterns: Make sure your code is readable
assertions.description: Should remove self-evident instructions Claude follows by default

assertions.name: keeps-useful-commands
assertions.description: Must preserve the actual build/test commands

assertions.name: keeps-migration-command
assertions.description: Must preserve the database migration command (non-obvious)

evals.name: migrate-from-cursorrules
evals.prompt:
I've been using Cursor and have a .cursorrules file. I'm switching to Claude Code and want to migrate my setup. Here's my .cursorrules:

```
You are an expert in TypeScript, Node.js, Next.js App Router, React, Shadcn UI, Radix UI, Tailwind.

Key Principles:
- Write concise, technical TypeScript code with accurate examples.
- Use functional and declarative programming patterns; avoid classes.
- Prefer iteration and modularization over code duplication.
- Use descriptive variable names with auxiliary verbs (e.g., isLoading, hasError).

Naming Conventions:
- Use lowercase with dashes for directories (e.g., components/auth-wizard).
- Favor named exports for components.

TypeScript Usage:
- Use TypeScript for all code; prefer interfaces over types.
- Avoid enums; use maps instead.
- Use functional components with TypeScript interfaces.

Syntax and Formatting:
- Use the 'function' keyword for pure functions.
- Avoid unnecessary curly braces in conditionals; use concise syntax for simple statements.

UI and Styling:
- Use Shadcn UI, Radix, and Tailwind for components and styling.
- Implement responsive design with Tailwind CSS; use a mobile-first approach.

Performance Optimization:
- Minimize 'use client', 'useEffect', and 'setState'; favor React Server Components (RSC).
- Wrap client components in Suspense with fallback.
- Use dynamic loading for non-critical components.
- Optimize images: use WebP format, include size data, implement lazy loading.
```

Can you turn this into a proper Claude Code setup?
evals.expected_output: A CLAUDE.md that keeps the genuinely useful rules (naming, TypeScript preferences, performance patterns) while removing things Claude already knows (standard TS/React patterns). Should NOT just copy the .cursorrules verbatim. Should add build/test commands that were missing from .cursorrules. May suggest rules files if appropriate.

assertions.name: claude-md-under-200-lines
assertions.description: CLAUDE.md must be under 200 lines

assertions.name: not-verbatim-copy
assertions.description: Output should NOT be a verbatim copy of .cursorrules content -- it should be restructured for Claude Code format

assertions.name: removes-teaching-content
assertions.description: Should remove role-playing preamble and generic principle headers from .cursorrules

assertions.name: keeps-project-specific-conventions
assertions.description: Should preserve genuinely non-obvious project conventions like directory naming

assertions.name: adds-verification-commands
assertions.description: Should add build/test/lint commands that were missing from .cursorrules

evals.name: rust-project-with-imports
evals.prompt: I have a Rust workspace with 4 crates. There's already a README.md with architecture docs and a CONTRIBUTING.md with git workflow. I don't want to duplicate that info in CLAUDE.md -- can you set it up so CLAUDE.md just imports those files and adds the stuff Claude specifically needs?
evals.expected_output: A CLAUDE.md that uses @README.md and @CONTRIBUTING.md import syntax to reference existing docs, and only adds Claude-specific instructions (cargo commands, test commands, workspace-specific quirks). Should be very short since most content is imported.

assertions.name: uses-import-syntax
assertions.description: Must use @import syntax to reference README.md

assertions.name: uses-contributing-import
assertions.description: Must use @import syntax to reference CONTRIBUTING.md

assertions.name: very-short
assertions.description: With imports handling most content, CLAUDE.md itself should be very short

assertions.name: has-cargo-commands
assertions.description: Must include cargo build/test/clippy commands

assertions.name: no-architecture-duplication
assertions.description: Should NOT repeat architecture info that's already in README.md -- the import handles that

evals.name: add-agent-to-existing-setup
evals.prompt: I already have a solid CLAUDE.md and some rules files. I want to add a security-review agent that can check PRs for common vulnerabilities -- SQL injection, XSS, hardcoded secrets, insecure deserialization. It should be read-only (no file edits) and use the opus model for deeper analysis. Can you create just the agent file?
evals.expected_output: A single .claude/agents/security-reviewer.md file with correct YAML frontmatter (name, description, tools restricted to read-only, model: opus) and a focused system prompt covering the specified vulnerability categories.

assertions.name: creates-agent-file-only
assertions.description: Should only create the agent file, not modify existing CLAUDE.md or rules

assertions.name: has-name-field
assertions.description: Must have name field in frontmatter

assertions.name: has-description-field
assertions.description: Must have description field

assertions.name: read-only-tools
assertions.description: Must specify tool restrictions

assertions.name: no-write-tools
assertions.description: Should NOT include Write or Edit in tools list (read-only requirement)

assertions.name: uses-opus-model
assertions.description: Must set model to opus as requested

assertions.name: covers-requested-vulns
assertions.description: System prompt must address the vulnerability categories the user specified
